metadata.title: [Re] Graph Edit Networks
organizations: University of Ljubljana, Faculty of Computer and Information Science

sections.title: Reproduced: Paaßen et. al. (ICLR, 2021)
content: The reproduced paper [bpa21] proposes a novel output layer for graph neural networks (GNNs), the graph edit network called (GEN). This layer yields a sequence of graph edits: node insertions, node deletions, node replacements, edge insertions and edge deletions.
The authors empirically underpin their theoretical claims about GENs by showing that they perform well in a series of graph time-series prediction tests.

figures.description: GEN model architecture showing original graph to predicted graph transformation

sections.title: DGPs & Tests
content: The paper evaluated several key claims on synthetic datasets, generated with data generating rules, interpreted in the following figures.

figures.description: Degree rules for node and edge insertions
Conway's Game of Life grid sequence
Peano Addition modelling addition problems via trees

sections.title: [Re] Original experimental claims
content: [bpa21] makes three experimental conclusions based on a series of experiments. Symbols below denote a successful reproduction of results.
✓ GENs outperform baselines (Variational Graph Autoencoders [KW16] & Variational Graph Recurrent nets [HHN19]) on time-series prediction tasks.
✓ GENs achieve 100% accuracy on time-series prediction tasks on graphs, generated from Boolean Formulae & Peano Addition DGPs.
? The runtime of forward passes of a GEN scales sub-quadratically as the number of nodes in a graph increases.

tables.description: Log-log linear slope fit for runtime scaling

figures.description: Runtime of forward and backward passes vs graph node count
Training loss over iterations for different parameters

sections.title: Improving the experimental setup
content: The facts that graph-to-graph transitions are deterministic and that the space of unique graphs that may be generated by the DGPs is limited, arose some concern about the experimental setup.
Increased cardinality of test time series (5 -> 100)
Ensured no duplicates between train & test set
Used established random graph generation methods
Results were generally worse, but still within a reasonable margin of error.

tables.description: Number of unique graphs generated by different DGPs
Probability of sampling unique and simplifyable trees

figures.description: Distribution of sampled TS lengths for boolean and peano

sections.title: Concluding reproduction notes
content: Our experimental results conclusively show that most of the claims in the original work hold. However, our work beyond the original paper emphasizes the need to pay attention to the experimental setup.

sections.title: References
content: [bpa21] Paaßen, Benjamin, et al. "Graph edit networks." ICLR 2021.
[Gar70] Martin Gardner. "The fantastic combinations of John Conway's new solitaire game of life." 1970.
[KW16] Thomas N. Kipf and Max Welling. "Variational graph auto-encoders." 2016.
[HHN19] Hajiramezanali, Ehsan, et. al. "Variational graph recurrent neural networks." NeurIPS 2019.
